title: Red Hill Market  -  First Saturday, Every Month
summary: Victoria's largest artisan craft market. 200+ stalls, the whole ridge in one place, and a morning that pulls the entire Peninsula into orbit.
kidsGradeNote: Bring a pram, bring a six-year-old, bring a teenager  -  the market is genuinely everyone-friendly. Space to wander, food stalls everywhere, plant sellers double as a soft play area for the small ones.
editorVerdict: If you only ever do one Peninsula market, do this one. Everything else is an understudy.
editorNote:
Red Hill Market runs the first Saturday of the month from September through May and it is the only Peninsula market that genuinely earns the hype. 200+ stalls, proper makers (not reseller tat), a coffee queue worth joining, and enough produce, plants and small-run ceramics to replace a Sunday afternoon shop at a fancy deli.

The single best move: arrive 8:45am, park at the recreation reserve while it's still possible, do the food lap first, the craft lap second, the plants lap last. By 11am it's packed. By 12:30 it's over. Budget two hours, eat as you go, and drive up the ridge for an early lunch before the cellar doors fill.

The months to beat the crowds: October, March, May. The months to skip: any December weekend the market coincides with school-holiday traffic  -  park outside Red Hill village and walk in.
